name: Menna v. New York
summary: The Court ruled that a guilty plea does not waive a defendant's double jeopardy claim because such a claim goes to the constitutional power of the government to hale the defendant into court at all.
significance: Held that a double jeopardy claim survives a guilty plea because it challenges the government's power to prosecute at all, supporting Hunter's argument that his double jeopardy claim is not waived by his plea.
